commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is a maintainer compatibility decision, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff changes media runner file-output handling, tests, and docs; I found no concrete security or supply-chain regression. (sha=658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes inferred transcript-file outputs authoritative for selected CLI audio transcription paths and updates media-understanding tests plus parakeet docs.

PR surface: Source +30, Tests +337, Docs 0. Total +367 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reads inferred transcript files only when non-empty and otherwise falls through to stdout. I did not run a local failing repro because this review was read-only, but the PR includes live after-fix logs for the central path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is a maintainer compatibility decision, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(media): suppress local whisper progress transcripts
target.body:
## What

CLI audio transcription now treats deterministic transcript-file outputs as authoritative instead of falling back to progress/status stdout.

This covers:

- `whisper-cli` with `-otxt` / `--output-txt` and `-of` / `--output-file`
- `whisper` with `--output_format txt` and `--output_dir`
- `parakeet-mlx` with explicit `--output-format txt` and `--output-dir`
- the local Whisper Node wrapper output paths via `--output-dir`, `--output`, and `-o`

Empty or missing inferred transcript files now produce no transcript instead of exposing status/progress output as user text. Unexpected transcript-file read errors still surface. Other Node wrappers, parakeet default/non-txt output, and other non-authoritative CLI paths keep the existing stdout fallback behavior.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87384.

## Why

Some local speech-to-text wrappers print progress banners to stdout while writing the actual transcript to a file. When that transcript file is empty or missing, OpenClaw could previously treat the progress banner as the user transcript. That is wrong for live audio messages because wrapper status text can leak into the inbound conversation body.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: local Whisper-style CLI wrapper status text could be injected as an audio transcript when the expected transcript file was empty or missing.
- Real environment tested: disposable OpenClaw staging gateway from this PR branch, Telegram provider `@kesslerClawBot`, inbound Telegram DM voice message (`audio/ogg`), and a controlled local-whisper-compatible wrapper that printed Whisper progress to stdout, wrote an empty inferred transcript file under `{{OutputDir}}`, and exited successfully.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the disposable staging gateway from PR head `ec4e753514ca371de8fe62d6d0fd88968d14e730`, sent a Telegram DM voice message to `@kesslerClawBot`, then ran a direct staging CLI audio transcription check against the same empty-output wrapper setup. current head `658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980` merges current `origin/main` and keeps the local-whisper runtime files proven by the staging run unchanged since the proof run.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): public proof comment with redacted live gateway transcript and direct CLI output: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87393#issuecomment-4558254748

  ```txt
  [telegram] starting provider (@kesslerClawBot)
  [telegram] update: {... "voice":{"duration":6,"mime_type":"audio/ogg","file_size":125925}}
  audio-preflight: transcribing attachment 0 for mention check
  Media understanding via CLI: node .../proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js .../media/inbound/<redacted>.ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-<redacted>
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output
  telegram inbound: ... preview="[Telegram ...] <media:audio>"
  Media understanding via CLI: node .../proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js .../media/inbound/<redacted>.ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-<redacted>
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output

  Direct staging CLI check:
  Error: No transcript returned for audio: /tmp/openclaw-whisper-proof-<redacted>/sample.wav
  ```

- Observed result after fix: OpenClaw invoked media understanding for the real Telegram voice note, logged the wrapper progress output, reported `Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output`, and kept the inbound Telegram body as `<media:audio>`. The progress stdout was not injected as transcript text.
- What was not tested: real Whisper model transcription quality and real upstream `parakeet-mlx` binary execution were not tested in the staging proof. The parakeet compatibility path is covered by focused regression tests instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the staging proof uses a controlled wrapper to force the empty-output condition deterministically. It proves OpenClaw's wrapper-output handling in the live gateway/Telegram path, not model accuracy.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): openclaw/openclaw#87384 describes the reported failure mode; current main fell back from missing/empty inferred transcript files to stdout for known CLI paths.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts
git diff --check
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose -t "auto-detects whisper-cli|transcodes non-wav audio"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts -t "parakeet|whisper-cli inferred|inferred whisper transcript|local whisper|node wrapper"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Result:

- formatting passed
- diff whitespace check passed
- focused CI-regression media tests passed: 2 tests
- focused parakeet/Whisper/local-wrapper tests passed: 9 tests, 4 skipped
- broader media-understanding audio files passed: 65 tests
- core TypeScript check passed

## CI Status

CI is running on current head `658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980`.

The previous `checks-node-core-runtime-media-ui` failure was PR-caused: two auto-detected `whisper-cli` tests still expected stdout fallback even though this PR makes inferred transcript files authoritative. The current branch updates those tests to create the inferred `-of ... .txt` transcript file and passes the local equivalent.

The previous `Real behavior proof` failure was a PR-body parser failure: the proof content was present, but the body did not use the exact required field labels. This body now uses the template labels parsed by `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`.

The later `checks-fast-bundled-protocol` and `checks-node-agentic-agents` failures on `f85508ba3b06a38a93d053ec545415f146ba6cdd` were stale-main failures outside this PR's media surface. The branch is now merged with `origin/main` at `53ad531df9`; local reruns passed:

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts -t "builds an openai fallback for gpt-5.5 when the live catalog cache is cold"`
- `pnpm test:bundled`
- `pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/nodes/audio.md docs/nodes/media-understanding.md src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Maintainer Edits

Maintainer edits are enabled on this fork PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted implementation and review. Human-run proof was captured through the disposable staging gateway and posted publicly in the proof comment above. Local review included scoped simplify/code-review passes and the repo autoreview helper; a later ad-hoc `codex review --base origin/main` attempt timed out before producing a final verdict, so I am not treating that as a completed clean review.

---

[![Compound Engineering](https://img.shields.io/badge/Built_with-Compound_Engineering-6366f1)](https://github.com/EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin)
![Codex](https://img.shields.io/badge/Codex-000000)

  ```txt
  Error: No transcript returned for audio: /tmp/openclaw-whisper-proof-<redacted>/sample.wav
  ```

- Observed result after fix: OpenClaw invoked media understanding for the real Telegram voice note, logged the wrapper progress output, reported `Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output`, and kept the inbound Telegram body as `<media:audio>`. The progress stdout was not injected as transcript text.
- What was not tested: real Whisper model transcription quality and real upstream `parakeet-mlx` binary execution were not tested in the staging proof. The parakeet compatibility path is covered by focused regression tests instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the staging proof uses a controlled wrapper to force the empty-output condition deterministically. It proves OpenClaw's wrapper-output handling in the live gateway/Telegram path, not model accuracy.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): openclaw/openclaw#87384 describes the reported failure mode; current main fell back from missing/empty inferred transcript files to stdout for known CLI paths.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts
git diff --check
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose -t "auto-detects whisper-cli|transcodes non-wav audio"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts -t "parakeet|whisper-cli inferred|inferred whisper transcript|local whisper|node wrapper"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Result:

- formatting passed
- diff whitespace check passed
- focused CI-regression media tests passed: 2 tests
- focused parakeet/Whisper/local-wrapper tests passed: 9 tests, 4 skipped
- broader media-understanding audio files passed: 65 tests
- core TypeScript check passed

## CI Status

CI is running on current head `658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980`.

The previous `checks-node-core-runtime-media-ui` failure was PR-caused: two auto-detected `whisper-cli` tests still expected stdout fallback even though this PR makes inferred transcript files authoritative. The current branch updates those tests to create the inferred `-of ... .txt` transcript file and passes the local equivalent.

The previous `Real behavior proof` failure was a PR-body parser failure: the proof content was present, but the body did not use the exact required field labels. This body now uses the template labels parsed by `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`.

The later `checks-fast-bundled-protocol` and `checks-node-agentic-agents` failures on `f85508ba3b06a38a93d053ec545415f146ba6cdd` were stale-main failures outside this PR's media surface. The branch is now merged with `origin/main` at `53ad531df9`; local reruns passed:

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts -t "builds an openai fallback for gpt-5.5 when the live catalog cache is cold"`
- `pnpm test:bundled`
- `pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/nodes/audio.md docs/nodes/media-understanding.md src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Maintainer Edits

Maintainer edits are enabled on this fork PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted implementation and review. Human-run proof was captured through the disposable staging gateway and posted publicly in the proof comment above. Local review included scoped simplify/code-review passes and the repo autoreview helper; a later ad-hoc `codex review --base origin/main` attempt timed out before producing a final verdict, so I am not treating that as a completed clean review.

commands.author: kesslerio

target.title: fix(media): suppress local whisper progress transcripts
target.body:
## What

CLI audio transcription now treats deterministic transcript-file outputs as authoritative instead of falling back to progress/status stdout.

This covers:

- `whisper-cli` with `-otxt` / `--output-txt` and `-of` / `--output-file`
- `whisper` with `--output_format txt` and `--output_dir`
- `parakeet-mlx` with explicit `--output-format txt` and `--output-dir`
- the local Whisper Node wrapper output paths via `--output-dir`, `--output`, and `-o`

Empty or missing inferred transcript files now produce no transcript instead of exposing status/progress output as user text. Unexpected transcript-file read errors still surface. Other Node wrappers, parakeet default/non-txt output, and other non-authoritative CLI paths keep the existing stdout fallback behavior.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#87384.

## Why

Some local speech-to-text wrappers print progress banners to stdout while writing the actual transcript to a file. When that transcript file is empty or missing, OpenClaw could previously treat the progress banner as the user transcript. That is wrong for live audio messages because wrapper status text can leak into the inbound conversation body.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: local Whisper-style CLI wrapper status text could be injected as an audio transcript when the expected transcript file was empty or missing.
- Real environment tested: disposable OpenClaw staging gateway from this PR branch, Telegram provider `@kesslerClawBot`, inbound Telegram DM voice message (`audio/ogg`), and a controlled local-whisper-compatible wrapper that printed Whisper progress to stdout, wrote an empty inferred transcript file under `{{OutputDir}}`, and exited successfully.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the disposable staging gateway from PR head `ec4e753514ca371de8fe62d6d0fd88968d14e730`, sent a Telegram DM voice message to `@kesslerClawBot`, then ran a direct staging CLI audio transcription check against the same empty-output wrapper setup. current head `658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980` merges current `origin/main` and keeps the local-whisper runtime files proven by the staging run unchanged since the proof run.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): public proof comment with redacted live gateway transcript and direct CLI output: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87393#issuecomment-4558254748

  ```txt
  [telegram] starting provider (@kesslerClawBot)
  [telegram] update: {... "voice":{"duration":6,"mime_type":"audio/ogg","file_size":125925}}
  audio-preflight: transcribing attachment 0 for mention check
  Media understanding via CLI: node .../proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js .../media/inbound/<redacted>.ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-<redacted>
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output
  telegram inbound: ... preview="[Telegram ...] <media:audio>"
  Media understanding via CLI: node .../proof/local-whisper/transcribe.js .../media/inbound/<redacted>.ogg --output-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-media-cli-<redacted>
  Whisper Voice Transcription
  Model: proof
  Transcribing with Whisper...
  Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output

  Direct staging CLI check:
  Error: No transcript returned for audio: /tmp/openclaw-whisper-proof-<redacted>/sample.wav
  ```

- Observed result after fix: OpenClaw invoked media understanding for the real Telegram voice note, logged the wrapper progress output, reported `Media understanding audio: skipped (0/1) reason=empty output`, and kept the inbound Telegram body as `<media:audio>`. The progress stdout was not injected as transcript text.
- What was not tested: real Whisper model transcription quality and real upstream `parakeet-mlx` binary execution were not tested in the staging proof. The parakeet compatibility path is covered by focused regression tests instead.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the staging proof uses a controlled wrapper to force the empty-output condition deterministically. It proves OpenClaw's wrapper-output handling in the live gateway/Telegram path, not model accuracy.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): openclaw/openclaw#87384 describes the reported failure mode; current main fell back from missing/empty inferred transcript files to stdout for known CLI paths.

## Validation

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts
git diff --check
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose -t "auto-detects whisper-cli|transcodes non-wav audio"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts -t "parakeet|whisper-cli inferred|inferred whisper transcript|local whisper|node wrapper"
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.media-understanding.config.ts --reporter=verbose src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts
timeout -k5s 180s node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Result:

- formatting passed
- diff whitespace check passed
- focused CI-regression media tests passed: 2 tests
- focused parakeet/Whisper/local-wrapper tests passed: 9 tests, 4 skipped
- broader media-understanding audio files passed: 65 tests
- core TypeScript check passed

## CI Status

CI is running on current head `658e605b17fdada9b985d2c3902cd3d1ebe1b980`.

The previous `checks-node-core-runtime-media-ui` failure was PR-caused: two auto-detected `whisper-cli` tests still expected stdout fallback even though this PR makes inferred transcript files authoritative. The current branch updates those tests to create the inferred `-of ... .txt` transcript file and passes the local equivalent.

The previous `Real behavior proof` failure was a PR-body parser failure: the proof content was present, but the body did not use the exact required field labels. This body now uses the template labels parsed by `scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-policy.mjs`.

The later `checks-fast-bundled-protocol` and `checks-node-agentic-agents` failures on `f85508ba3b06a38a93d053ec545415f146ba6cdd` were stale-main failures outside this PR's media surface. The branch is now merged with `origin/main` at `53ad531df9`; local reruns passed:

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts -t "builds an openai fallback for gpt-5.5 when the live catalog cache is cold"`
- `pnpm test:bundled`
- `pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/nodes/audio.md docs/nodes/media-understanding.md src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.cli-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Maintainer Edits

Maintainer edits are enabled on this fork PR.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted implementation and review. Human-run proof was captured through the disposable staging gateway and posted publicly in the proof comment above. Local review included scoped simplify/code-review passes and the repo autoreview helper; a later ad-hoc `codex review --base origin/main` attempt timed out before producing a final verdict, so I am not treating that as a completed clean review.
